Meta.tagline: Scrim operations tool for SF6 team battles
Meta.description: SFScrim is a scrim operations tool for SF6 team battles. Lineup input, home & away flow, and victory point tracking — all in the browser, fully aligned with the official SFL ruleset.
Meta.ogImageAlt: SFScrim — Scrim operations tool for SF6 team battles

scrim.title: Scrim dashboard
scrim.description: Manage lineups, match progression, and victory points for your scrim.

orderInput.title: Lineup input
orderInput.description: Enter the 1st / 2nd / 3rd lineup plus the tiebreak player.

broadcast.title: Broadcast view
broadcast.description: Stream-optimized scoreboard & lineup. Home's un-announced positions are auto-masked.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How SFScrim handles personal and usage information.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Send feedback, bug reports, and feature requests for SFScrim.

Landing.heroLead: SFScrim is a scrim operations tool for SF6 team battles. Lineup input, home & away flow, victory point tracking — all in the browser. Stats and match-history aggregation are next on the roadmap.

order.title: Lineup management
order.desc: 1st / 2nd / 3rd + tiebreak player. Away-first → home response is automated.

rules.title: Official ruleset
rules.desc: Playoff 70pt / Grand Final 90pt auto-switching. Mandatory appearance tracking built-in.

fourVsFour.title: 4v4 ready
fourVsFour.desc: Built-in support for the international 4v4 format.

i18n.title: Multilingual
i18n.desc: Instant switch between Japanese and English. Ready for international pros.

Order.flowExplanation: Away submits players & characters first → Home responds. Home cannot input until Away is locked.
Order.previewSaveHint: Save the lineup first, then preview.
Order.roleMismatchBody: You started this scrim as a different role. Opening the {role} view may reveal the opponent's lineup. Are you sure?
Order.notAnnouncedJumpHint: Click to open Home's lineup input
Order.sequentialIntro: Home announces one position at a time. Announce 1st → after 1st battle, announce 2nd → and so on.
Order.waitingForPrevious: Editable after the previous position is announced
Order.startScrimHint: Fill in vanguard / midfield / champion for both teams to enable.
Order.inProgressNotice: Lineup locked. Use the score input below to record match progress.

Score.regularDescription: 1 section = 1st → 2nd → 3rd in a single round (max {max}pt). A tie goes to a tiebreak (Bo3 / +{tiebreak}pt).
Score.tiebreakSubMissing: Register a tiebreak player on both teams

Broadcast.hint: Home's un-announced positions are auto-masked. Safe to capture in OBS without leaking lineup info.

privacy.intro: SFScrim (the "Service") is a web app that supports team-battle operations for Street Fighter 6. This policy describes how the Service handles personal and usage information.

sections.heading: 1. Information we collect
sections.body:
- Inputs: scrim lineups, scores, and similar data are stored in your browser (localStorage) and not sent to our servers by default.
- Access logs: source IP address, User-Agent, and similar metadata are recorded as Cloudflare Workers access logs.
- Authentication info (after sign-in is released): when using Discord / Google OAuth, we obtain your display name, avatar URL, and email address.

sections.heading: 2. How we use the information
sections.body:
- To provide and improve the Service
- To detect abuse and to maintain security
- To respond to your inquiries

sections.heading: 3. Sharing with third parties
sections.body: Except as required by law, we do not share collected information with third parties.

sections.heading: 4. Third-party services
sections.body:
The Service may use the following third-party services:
- Cloudflare (infrastructure)
- Discord / Google (OAuth authentication)
- Google AdSense (advertising, after approval)

sections.heading: 5. Cookies
sections.body: We may use cookies to maintain your authenticated session. Cookies used for advertising are handled in accordance with Google's policies.

sections.heading: 6. Contact
sections.body: For questions about this policy, please reach out via the Contact page.

sections.heading: 7. Changes
sections.body: This policy may be updated without prior notice. Updates take effect when published on this page.

terms.intro: These Terms of Service (the "Terms") govern use of SFScrim (the "Service"). By using the Service, you agree to these Terms.

sections.heading: 1. Scope
sections.body: These Terms apply to everyone who uses the Service.

sections.heading: 2. Use of the Service
sections.body:
- The Service is an individually operated tool for scrim operations and is provided on an as-is basis. Use at your own risk.
- Input data is stored in your browser (localStorage) by default. We are not responsible for data loss.

sections.heading: 3. Prohibited conduct
sections.body:
You must not:
- Violate any law or public order and morals
- Interfere with the operation of the Service or attempt to reverse-engineer it
- Impersonate any third party

sections.heading: 4. Intellectual property
sections.body: The Service's source code is published on GitHub. Information about the Street Fighter League (SFL) shown in the Service is sourced from official CAPCOM materials.

sections.heading: 5. Disclaimer
sections.body: The Service is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranty. We are not liable for any damage arising from use of the Service.

sections.heading: 6. Relationship with SFL / CAPCOM
sections.body: The Service is not affiliated with the Street Fighter League (SFL) or CAPCOM Co., Ltd. in any way.

sections.heading: 7. Changes to these Terms
sections.body: These Terms may be updated without prior notice.

contact.intro: For feedback, bug reports, and feature requests for SFScrim, please open a thread on GitHub Issues.
contact.responseHint: This is an individually operated project — replies may take some time. Thanks for your patience.
